The decisive factor is Clemson's significant injury loss: starting center Carter Welling tore his ACL in the ACC Tournament and is out for the season, depriving the Tigers of their interior presence (10.4 PPG, 5.5 RPG) and compromising the defense that ranks 20th nationally. While Clemson showed resilience by beating North Carolina without Welling, they subsequently fell to Duke by 12, and Iowa's high-powered offense should exploit the shorthanded Tigers in what projects as a close but winnable 8/9 matchup.

result.text: Carter Welling injury, Clemson center tears ACL, done for season Skip to main content Home Olympics NFL NCAAB NCAAW NBA Women's Sports NCAAF MLB WNBA Sports Betting Big Ten Sports SEC Sports Boxing Coaches Salaries Draft Hub Fantasy Football For The Win Formula 1 Golfweek High School Sports High School Sports Awards IndyCar MMA NASCAR National Signing Day NHL Soccer Sports Business Sports Pulse Sports Seriously Tennis WWE Youth Sports Outdoors Fantasy Bet With the People Clear Play Arizona Sports Indiana Sports Michigan Sports Ohio Sports Tennessee Sports Wisconsin Teams For Subscribers Carter Welling injury update, Clemson basketball center out for season with torn ACL Derrian Carter Greenville News CHARLOTTE, NC — Clemson basketball starting center Carter Welling suffered a torn ACL in his right knee and is out for the season, the school announced March 12. An MRI confirmed the ACL tear, and there is no timetable for his return. He has one season of eligibility remaining. Welling injured his knee in No. 5 seed Clemson 's 71-62 win vs. the No. 12 Demon Deacons on March 11 in the ACC Tournament at Spectrum Center. Welling, a Utah Valley transfer, drove to the paint for a layup attempt between two Wake Forest defenders but fell and grabbed his right knee with 1:58 remaining in the first half. Welling needed assistance from two Clemson trainers to help him off the court. He went into the locker room and was ruled out for the remainder of the game. He returned to the bench late in the second half with a brace on his knee and on crutches. Welling is Clemson's second-leading scorer, averaging 10.2 points. He is also the Tigers' top rebounder (5.4 per game) and shot blocker (24 total). MORE: Clemson basketball bracketology live updates, predictions as Selection Sunday approaches With Welling out, Clemson will rely on RJ Godfrey, Nick Davidson and freshman Chase Thompson to step up for the rest of the postseason. This is the second season-ending injury Clemson has endured this season after freshman guard Zac Foster tore his ACL in December. It is also the second straight season where a Clemson player suffered a season-ending injury in the ACC Tournament after guard Dillon Hunter broke his hand last season. No. 5 seed Clemson (23-9) faces No. 4 North Carolina (24-7) on March 12 (9:30 p.m. ET, ESPN) at Spectrum Center in the ACC Tournament quarterfinals. An MRI confirmed the ACL tear, and there is no timetable for his return. He has one season of eligibility remaining.
